input.prompt:
I'm adding dark mode to my .NET MAUI Android app. I created ResourceDictionary
themes and used StaticResource to bind colors in my XAML pages. When I
switch themes at runtime nothing changes — the colors stay the same. Also,
when the user toggles dark mode in Android settings my app seems to restart
completely. What am I doing wrong?

input.initialPrompt:
I'm adding dark mode to my .NET MAUI Android app. I created ResourceDictionary
themes and used StaticResource to bind colors in my XAML pages. When I
switch themes at runtime nothing changes — the colors stay the same. Also,
when the user toggles dark mode in Android settings my app seems to restart
completely. What am I doing wrong?

data.content:
Two separate issues here:

**1. `StaticResource` won't update at runtime.**
`StaticResource` resolves **once** at load time. Use **`DynamicResource`** instead — it re-evaluates whenever the underlying resource changes:

```xml
<!-- ✗ won't update -->
<Label TextColor="{StaticResource PrimaryTextColor}" />

<!-- ✓ updates when you swap the merged dictionary -->
<Label TextColor="{DynamicResource PrimaryTextColor}" />
```

Then swap themes by replacing the merged dictionary at runtime:

```csharp
var mergedDicts = Application.Current.Resources.MergedDictionaries;
mergedDicts.Clear();
mergedDicts.Add(useDark ? new DarkTheme() : new LightTheme());
```

**2. Activity restart on Android `uiMode` change.**
Android destroys and recreates the `Activity` when system dark mode toggles. Tell it to handle the change itself by adding `UiMode` to `ConfigurationChanges` in your `MainActivity`:

```csharp
// Platforms/Android/MainActivity.cs
[Activity(
    Theme = "@style/Maui.SplashTheme",
    MainLauncher = true,
    ConfigurationChanges = ConfigChanges.ScreenSize
                         | ConfigChanges.Orientation
                         | ConfigChanges.UiMode       // ← add this
                         | ConfigChanges.ScreenLayout
                         | ConfigChanges.SmallestScreenSize
                         | ConfigChanges.Density)]
public class MainActivity : MauiAppCompatActivity { }
```

With `UiMode` handled, the activity won't restart. You can then react to the system change by overriding `OnConfigurationChanged` if you want to auto-follow the system setting:

```csharp
public override void OnConfigurationChanged(Configuration newConfig)
{
    base.OnConfigurationChanged(newConfig);

    bool systemIsDark = (newConfig.UiMode & UiMode.NightMask) == UiMode.NightYes;
    // swap your merged ResourceDictionary here
}
```

**TL;DR** — `StaticResource` → `DynamicResource` fixes the runtime swap, and adding `ConfigChanges.UiMode` stops the restart.
data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about two specific .NET MAUI issues related to theming/dark mode on Android:

1. **StaticResource vs DynamicResource**: They're using `StaticResource` to bind theme colors, but colors don't update at runtime when switching themes. This is because `StaticResource` is resolved once at load time and never updates. They need `DynamicResource` for runtime theme switching.

2. **App restart on Android system dark mode toggle**: When the user changes dark mode in Android settings, the app restarts. This is because Android recreates the Activity on configuration changes like `uiMode`. The fix is to handle this in the `MainActivity` by adding `ConfigChanges` to prevent the activity from being destroyed and recreated.


These are straightforward MAUI issues I can address directly without needing to examine any code.
